mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate over recent months tightens financial conditions and raises equity discount rates, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days turn negative when policy is moving upward.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of months where FEDFUNDS is rising for 2 consecutive observations, or if rolling 3-year rank IC stays above 0.03 with the wrong sign.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in UNRATE above trend signals weakening household income and rising recession risk, which compresses near-term equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the triggered-minus-untriggered return spread is not negative at 5% significance.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales accelerate and housing starts stabilize together, the market reads stronger domestic demand and better earnings momentum, so S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to outperform either proxy alone in out-of-sample tests, or if 5-day forward returns after simultaneous strength in RSAFS and HOUST are not positive on average.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and operating leverage, which lifts cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if breakout episodes in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal's rolling IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Low CPI inflation combined with very low FEDFUNDS implies real rates remain contained and liquidity conditions are supportive, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation falls below 2% and FEDFUNDS stays below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the low-inflation/low-rate regime does not outperform all other regimes on 60-day forward returns, or if the conditional mean return is not positive at conventional significance levels.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS level during a high-UNRATE regime confirms labor-market deterioration and weaker earnings power, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous payroll declines and elevated unemployment do not produce negative 20-day returns on average, or if the joint signal is not stronger than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth signals improving aggregate demand and revenue growth, so the S&P 500 should earn positive returns over the next 60 trading days after a GDP acceleration.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP acceleration is not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the spread versus non-acceleration periods is statistically zero.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in housing starts and retail sales marks weakening consumer demand and construction activity, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both fall below their 3-month averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in housing and retail does not predict negative 5-day returns, or if triggered instances yield positive returns in at least 60% of cases.
